Job Responsibilities:

1. Daily Laboratory Operations Management:

  •  Assist in the smooth execution of daily activities within the laboratory.
  • Ensure the effective functioning of all laboratory equipment and instruments.
  • Support laboratory staff in maintaining efficient workflows and processes.

2. Equipment and Process Maintenance:

  •  Ensure regular inspection, maintenance, and calibration of laboratory equipment.
  • Identify and troubleshoot any malfunctioning or issues with laboratory instruments and materials.
  • Coordinate repairs or replacements when necessary to maintain optimal operation.

3. Documentation and Record Keeping:

  •  Accurately document and record all information related to tests, results, and laboratory processes.
  • Ensure proper storage of test records and patient information following established protocols.
  • Maintain an organized system for easy retrieval of data and reports.

4. Team Coordination:

  •  Assist in coordinating daily tasks and activities among laboratory team members.
  • Ensure seamless collaboration among lab personnel for efficient operations.
  • Provide support to team members as needed, ensuring smooth workflow.

5. Quality Assurance and Standards:

  • Regularly inspect equipment, structures, and materials to identify and address potential causes of errors or inconsistencies.
  • Help implement and uphold high-quality standards for analytical test performance.
  •  Participate in quality control processes and regular testing to ensure accurate results.

6. Test Performance and Adherence to SOPs:

  • Support all stages of test performance, ensuring compliance with pre-analytic, analytic, and post-analytic standards.
  • Strictly follow standard operating procedures (SOPs) to ensure consistency and accuracy.
  • Deliver quality service in all laboratory tasks, maintaining high accuracy in every phase of testing.

7. Confidentiality and Compliance:

  • Uphold patient confidentiality in accordance with industry standards and organizational policies.
  •  Ensure proper handling, documentation, and reporting of sensitive patient information.

Interested candidates may Send their application at hr@nationalpathlabs.com

along with their updated CV

Position: Program Coordinator – STEP Project

Location: Surkhet, Karnali

Department: Blink Now Nepal Programs

Employment Type: Full-Time

ABOUT THE ORGANIZATION

“A world where every child is educated, safe, and loved”

The BlinkNow Foundation is a 501(c)(3) based in New Jersey which, through its country office in Nepal, oversees programs in rural Nepal including a school, a children’s home, and a women’s training center. We do this with our local implementing partner, Kopila Valley Sewa Samaj, and through close ties with the local community. Our mission is to change the world by empowering Nepal’s children.

STEP (Strengthening Public Schools Through Partnerships) is BlinkNow Foundation’s education initiative focused on improving learning outcomes in public schools through strategic partnerships, teacher capacity building, curriculum strengthening, systems and infrastructure support.

POSITION SUMMARY

The Program Coordinator – STEP Project plays an important role in strengthening public schools education in Karnali Province by supporting increased enrollment, retention, and learning outcomes in partner public schools, with a focus on children from socially marginalized and economically disadvantaged communities. The role coordinates government and non-government partnerships to introduce innovative, scalable practices into the public education system.

By working closely with the implementing partner, local governments,, school leadership, teachers, communities, and BlinkNow teams, the Coordinator contributes to improved access to quality education, reduced repetition and dropout rates, enhanced teacher and institutional capacity, improved learning environments, and the establishment of a documented, replicable public–private partnership model for strengthening public sector education.

MANAGEMENT STRUCTURE

Reporting To: Country Representative

Direct Reports: NA (but will need to directly oversee the work of relevant team members from the implementing partner)

Work Location: Surkhet, Karnali Region

Must Haves

  • Education: Bachelor’s degree in relevant discipline
  • Experience: At least 4 years of proven experience in the relevant field (minimum 2 years in a supervisory role)
  • Language: A good communicator, and fluent in English and Nepali
  • Skills and Abilities: Proactive initiator; strong coordination skills; passionate about and committed to caring for children and vulnerable populations; and proficient in using computer tools.
    Works collaboratively and independently with the least supervision required.

CORE RESPONSIBILITIES

  • Coordinate with the Stakeholders for effective running of the STEPS Project.
  • Ensure the outcomes set for the STEPS project are achieved in a timely manner.
  • Work with the relevant team members for smooth implementation of the STEPS project.

DETAILED RESPONSIBILITIES

Coordinate with the Stakeholders for effective running of the STEPS Project.

  • Coordinate with the municipality for alignment, approval, and sustainability of the project.
  • Oversee and closely support the work of implementing partner’s team members assigned to the
  • STEPS work. Make sure the seamless implementation of the project.
  • Work closely with the school management committee for their ownership of the change initiative.
  • Keep the school principals abreast of the planned activities and ensure they lead and continue the program.
  • Support school teacher and team on the day to day execution of the planned activities towards improved education outlined in the project.
  • Build collaboration with the like minded organizations working in the area of school education improvement.

Ensure the goals and outcomes set for the STEPS project are achieved in a timely manner.

  • Increased student enrollment and attendance at the partner schools.
  • Learning outcomes of the students improved, measured through EGMA, EGRA, and other standardized tests conducted at the beginning and end of the project period.
  • Enhanced the Capacity of public-school teachers, SMC, and PTA for better learning improvement.
  • Improved public school infrastructure for a better learning environment for children.
  • Improved institutional capacity of selected public schools, including filling human resource gaps, co-curricular interventions, and quality assessment mechanisms.
  • Documentation of learning improvement and knowledge management process.

Work with the relevant team members for smooth implementation of the STEPS project.

  • Coordinate the gap identification in the areas of intervention set forth by the project including, school selection, teaching pedagogy, resources, training, staffing, and provide regular feedback to the school team on progress and challenges.
  • Guide the school based team from the implementing partners and support teachers to strengthen classroom instruction and language learning opportunities.
  • Schedule and actively participate in regular separate and joint meetings with the school leadership to review, plan and execute on the plans.
  • Execure on all the activities listed in the project agreement and also those identified through the needs assessment and agreed to implement.
  • Get approval on the planned activities from the line manager and report on a timely manner.
  • Work closely with the Monitoring & Evaluation (M&E) Manager to document, evaluate, and report progress from baseline to final evaluation.

ADDITIONAL RESPONSIBILITIES

  • Work closely with the relevant team members to implement school improvement goals aligned with BlinkNow Foundation’s strategic plan.
  • Organize academic enrichment activities within and between the partner schools. Support BlinkNow Communications Team by contributing to social media and blog content related to the STEP Project.
  • Work closely with implementing partner teams to ensure effective program implementation, coordination, and execution at school and community levels.
  • Build positive working relationships within the organization, demonstrate professionalism, maintain confidentiality, and conduct regular one-on-one meetings with the line manager and direct reports.

KEY CHARACTERSTICS

  • You believe that we can change the world, in the blink of an eye
  • You believe in making decisions that put children first
  • You are self-motivated, organized, and approachable
  • You enjoy creating systems, keeping records, and increasing efficiency
  • You are always looking for new ways for our team to work smarter instead of harder
  • You are detail-oriented and conscientious. You make sure nothing slips through the cracks
  • You have a strong work ethic and a willingness to pick up new skills. You are comfortable multi-tasking
  • You are positive, patient, and a clear communicator who is comfortable working with relevant team
  • You are an empathetic person, with a knack for putting out fires before they start and soothing conflicts brewing beneath the surface
  • You require little supervision and are flexible. You understand that you will be wearing many hats in this role, many of which are not explicitly included in this job description

This job description is not exhaustive, the Program Coordinator for STEPS Project may be required to undertake additional responsibilities as assigned by the line manager, in line with project needs and organizational priorities.

Application procedure

If you think this opportunity will amplify your career growth, we encourage you to apply by clicking “Apply For This Job” and submitting all required documents.

Note: No telephone inquiries will be entertained. If your application is a good fit, we will follow up with you directly for the screening process.

Position: Economic & Policy Analyst

Reports To: Chief Financial Office

Job Purpose:

The Economic & Policy Analyst will serve as the Bank’s in-house economist and will be responsible for monitoring, interpreting, and assessing macroeconomic and policy developments relevant to the Bank and its subsidiaries. The role will provide timely and well-informed economic perspectives and data-driven insights to support decision-making at the strategic level.

This is a newly created role within Laxmi Sunrise, established to strengthen the Bank’s internal capacity for economic analysis. The role will also contribute to building the Bank’s understanding of regulatory and policy developments—both domestic and international—that have implications for business strategy, balance sheet management, and long-term direction.

Key Responsibilities:

  • Monitor and interpret macroeconomic and policy developments relevant to the Bank and its subsidiaries.
  • Assess the potential impact of economic and regulatory changes on the Bank’s strategy and operations.
  • Prepare regular updates and insights to support strategic planning and internal decision-making.
  • Collaborate with Treasury, Risk, Strategy, and Business teams to integrate economic perspectives into their planning processes.
  • Develop internal tools and resources to track economic trends.
  • Contribute to external communication materials where economic context is required.

Qualifications & Skills:

  • Minimum Master’s degree in Economics or a related discipline.
  • At least 3 years of relevant experience in economic research or policy advisory, ideally within a financial institution, multilateral agency, or research organization.
  • Strong understanding of Nepal’s macroeconomic environment and financial sector.
  • Proven ability to analyze economic and policy developments and translate them into practical insights for business decisions.
  • Strong analytical, written, and verbal communication skills.

Last day of application is 14th February, 2026 – applications after the date will not be accepted. Only shortlisted candidate(s) will be granted an interview. The Bank reserves the right to reject any (or all) applications without any reason whatsoever.

Experience: 1 year experience in IT field
Contract of Employment: Full-time
Job Location: Head Office

Skills and Knowledge:

  • the ability to think logically
  • a good memory of how software and operating systems work
  • excellent listening and questioning skills, combined with the ability to interact confidently with clients to establish what the problem is and explain the solution
  • the ability to work well in a team
  • problem solving skills
  • a strong customer focus
  • the ability to prioritize your workload
  • Attention to detail.

Job Duties & Responsibilities:

Job Description:

  • AGENT, shall review and maintain all corporate and consumer clients’ internet connectivity and help review computer systems, systems network of clients and suggest solutions by coordinating with desktop support if required. AGENT Field shall be helping and assisting all corporate field agents on technical and other issues.
  • AGENT, shall also work within first or second-line support. The work is as much about understanding how information systems are used as applying technical knowledge related to Internet, fiber connectivity, LAN/WAN, computer hardware or software and networking systems.

Responsibilities

AGENT are mainly responsible for reporting the Corporate Field Support tasks on daily basis. AGENT shall be responsible for containing and corresponding with all the corporate clients FV tasks of the day and provide update to clients.

  • The AGENT shall ensure that all corporate FVs are accounted for the day. All corporate visits shall be properly diagnosed with proper solutions. Upon any exceptions the clients shall be informed.
  • Install and operate full internet connectivity at clients site
  • monitor and maintain computer systems and networks of the clients and Vianet
  • Provide technical assistance to marketing agent to acquire new installations.
  • talk to staff or clients through a series of actions, either face-to-face or over the phone, to help set up internet systems or resolve issues or provide solutions
  • Troubleshoot system and network problems, diagnosing and solving hardware, internet or software faults.
  • replace equipment/parts as required
  • provide support, including procedural documentation and relevant reports
  • follow diagrams and written instructions to repair a fault or set up a system
  • support the roll-out of new applications
  • respond within agreed time limits to call-outs
  • work continuously on a task until completion (or referral to third parties, if appropriate)
  • prioritize and manage many open cases at one time
  • rapidly establish a good working relationship with customers and other professionals, such as software developers
  • test and evaluate new technology
  • Engage in proactive visits of corporate customer’s weekly/monthly.
  • Provide monthly report on works done.

Reporting:

  • The report shall be send on daily basis on the following tasks:
  • Proactive Follow up
  • Desk top Support
  • FV Support with forms
  • Installation Visits with clients internal network
  • Any trainings
  • Daily Activities

National Co-operative Bank Ltd. (NCBL) is the only national-level bank dedicated to Nepal’s cooperative movement. Committed to promoting sustainable cooperatives and expanding financial access, NCBL delivers high-quality professional banking services with a personal touch to cooperatives across the country.

To further strengthen its leadership team, NCBL invites applications from qualified and dynamic professionals for the position.

Position: Deputy Chief Executive Officer (DCEO)

Required No.: 1

Job Profile

The Deputy CEO will support the Chief Executive Officer in strategic planning, goal setting, and ensuring effective implementation across the Bank. “Key responsibilities include:”

  • Assisting in formulating operational targets, timelines, and monitoring mechanisms.
  • Overseeing financial, administrative, and operational functions as assigned.
  • Ensuring optimal utilization of resources to achieve institutional goals.
  • Providing strategic and operational guidance to designated departments and units.
  • Monitoring departmental performance and ensuring compliance with policies, procedures, and regulatory standards.

Qualification and Experience

  • Master’s degree in management, Humanities (Economics) from a recognized university with a minimum of second division or equivalent.
  • Professional Experience: Minimum 7 years of managerial-level experience. {Managerial level includes Manager or above (Commercial Banks); CEO or up to two levels below CEO (B, C, D Class FIs & NCBL); Executive Chief (Cooperatives);
  • Undersecretary or above (Government Organizations); or equivalent level as determined by the Recruitment Subcommittee.}
  • Age: Not exceeding 58 years. Preference will be given to younger, energetic candidates.
  • Salary & Benefits: Negotiable (Salary will not be a bar for deserving candidates)
  • Selection Process: Shortlisted candidates will be called for further selection.

Application Instructions

Interested candidates meeting the above criteria are requested to email their application along with required documents to vacancy@ncbl.coop within 15 days from the date of publication.

Document Checklist

  • Cover letter including your expected salary
  • Updated curriculum vitae (CV) /Resume
  • Copies of relevant academic certificates and transcripts
  • For academic qualifications obtained from foreign universities, an equivalency certificate issued by the competent authority in Nepal
  • Copy of Nepali Citizenship Certificate

Applicants who applied under the first notice need not reapply, as previously submitted documents will be considered. Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted. The Bank reserves the right to accept or reject any or all applications without assigning any reason. Canvassing at any stage will result in disqualification.

Position: Senior Accountant
Location: Nagarkot

Job Description

Job Summary

The Senior Accountant is responsible for managing day-to-day accounting operations, statutory compliance, tax and VAT filings, payroll processing, and accurate financial reporting for the resort. The role ensures timely documentation, strong internal control, and proper handling of cash and financial records. This position supports the finance function by maintaining accurate data, preparing reports, and assisting management in effective financial planning and decision-making.

Key Responsibilities

1. Accounting Operations & Reporting

  • Maintain accurate books of accounts, general ledger, and supporting records.
  • Prepare monthly financial reports including P&L, Balance Sheet, and cash flow summaries.
  •  Assist in month-end and year-end closing activities.
  • Reconcile bank accounts, vendor statements, and ledger balances regularly.
  • Ensure proper documentation and filing of all financial transactions.

2. Taxation & Statutory Compliance

  • Prepare and submit VAT returns, ETDS filings, and other statutory tax reports within deadlines.
  •  Ensure compliance with local tax laws, labor laws, and government regulations.
  • Maintain proper tax documentation and coordinate with auditors and tax authorities.
  •  Monitor tax payments, deductions, and reconciliations.

3. Payroll Management

  •  Process monthly payroll accurately and timely for all employees.
  •  Calculate salaries, allowances, overtime, deductions, and benefits.
  • Ensure statutory deductions are correctly applied and submitted.
  • Maintain employee payroll records and confidentiality.

4. Cash & Fund Management

  • Handle daily cash collection, disbursement, and reconciliation.
  •  Maintain petty cash and ensure proper approvals and documentation.
  •  Support cash flow monitoring to ensure smooth operations.
  •  Safeguard company funds and ensure accountability for all transactions.

5. Accounts Payable & Receivable

  •  Monitor vendor payments and customer receivables.
  •  Ensure timely billing, collections, and settlements.
  •  Maintain accurate aging reports and follow up on outstanding balances.

6. Documentation & Internal Controls

  • Maintain organized financial records, vouchers, invoices, and contracts.
    Follow internal control procedures and company financial policies.
  •  Assist in audits by providing required documents and reports.
  •  Identify and recommend improvements to accounting processes.

7. Systems & Excel Proficiency

  • Use accounting software and advanced MS Excel for reporting and analysis.
  • Prepare financial summaries, trackers, and dashboards using formulas and data tools.
  • Ensure accuracy and efficiency in data management.

8. Coordination & Support

  • Work closely with department heads to support financial requirements.
  •  Provide timely financial information and insights to management.

Pahichan Nepal is a national non-governmental organization (NGO) based in Kathmandu, Nepal, dedicated to empowering single and marginalized women with disabilities, particularly those who are unmarried, widowed, or divorced. As a self-help organization, Pahichan Nepal embodies a community-centered approach, empowering women with disabilities to advocate for their own rights and create sustainable change within their communities. The organization prioritizes inclusivity, dignity, and independence, creating a safe and supportive space where women can live, learn, and work free from stigma and discrimination.

Vision: To ensure that every single and marginalized woman with disabilities receives equal and dignified recognition in society.

Mission: To empower single and marginalized women with disabilities to rediscover their identity, live dignified lives, and build equitable communities.

Goal: To economically, socially, politically, and culturally empower single and marginalized women with disabilities.

Position: Finance Assistant (1)

Location: Kathmandu

Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary

Pahichan Nepal is seeking a responsible and detail-oriented Finance Assistant to support the organization’s finance and administrative functions. The Finance Assistant will work under the guidance of the Finance Officer/Manager and assist in maintaining financial records, processing payments, supporting budgeting and reporting, and ensuring compliance with organizational policies and the financial rules and regulations of Nepal. This role requires coordination with program and administrative teams to support smooth day-to-day operations.

Academic Qualification & Experience

  • Bachelor’s degree in Business Studies (BBS), Business Administration (BBA), or a related field
  • Minimum of 2 years of relevant experience in finance and administrative support roles, preferably in an NGO/INGO
  • Basic to intermediate proficiency in Microsoft Excel, Word, and PowerPoint, and familiarity with accounting software
  • Good communication skills and ability to work collaboratively in a team
  • Strong attention to detail, organizational skills, and willingness to learn

Major Duties and Responsibilities

Financial Record Keeping and Documentation

  • Assist in maintaining daily financial records and transactions
  • Prepare vouchers and supporting documents and enter data into accounting software
  • Maintain proper filing, archiving, and confidentiality of financial documents

Advance and Reconciliation Support

  • Assist in tracking advances, outstanding balances, and settlements
  • Support periodic bank and cash reconciliations under supervision
  • Follow up with staff and project teams for timely advance settlements

Payments and Budget Support

  • Assist in verifying payment requests and claims against approved budgets
  • Prepare payment documents, cheques, and bank transfer requests as instructed
  • Support budget tracking, expense monitoring, and basic budget forecasts

Compliance and Reporting Support

  • Assist in preparing documents for tax payments and statutory compliance
  • Support the preparation of basic financial reports and supplementary financial information
  • Help organize financial documents for audits and internal reviews

Administrative and Coordination Support

  • Provide administrative assistance related to project HR coordination and office operations
  • Coordinate with program teams to collect required financial documents and information
  • Participate in meetings, trainings, and internal discussions as required

Working Conditions

  • Occasional travel to project sites for financial and administrative support
  • Regular coordination with program staff and partner organizations at the field level

Salary & Benefits:

  • As per the rules and regulations of Pahichan Nepal.

How to Apply:

Interested candidates are invited to submit their CV and a cover letter detailing their qualifications and experience to pahichann@gmail.com by February 7, 2025.

We encourage applications from individuals of all backgrounds, including women, persons with disabilities, marginalized groups and are committed to creating an inclusive workplace.

Note: Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.

Position: Sponsorship Assistant
Location: Kanchanpur

With 75 years of experience, our focus is on helping the most vulnerable children overcome poverty and experience fullness of life. We help children of all backgrounds, even in the most dangerous places, inspired by our Christian faith.

Come join our 33,000+ staff working in nearly 100 countries and share the joy of transforming vulnerable children’s life stories!

Key Responsibilities:

MAJOR RESPONSIBILITIES

The Sponsorship Assistant provides support for the implementation of Sponsorship Business Operations within the AP. The Sponsorship Officer works closely with the AP team, partner organization, field mobilisers and community-based committees to ensure well-being of sponsored and non-sponsored children are in place and the organizational requirements related to Sponsorship operations are met according to Sponsorship standards and policies. This position will support to apply Sponsorship in Programming principles to ensure Sponsorship operations and processes integrate with the programme and community efforts: i) to care for and protect children; and ii) to strengthen children’s participation, so the registered children participate and benefit from programme activities.

All Sponsorship Systems are operated and updated efficiently

  • Ensure timely data entry in Horizon Systems regularly
  • Ensure data quality, validation, consolidation and qualification for Auto-APR process
  • Ensure all Horizon Hope mobile devices are synced regularly
  • Maintain management of mobile device as per guideline (MDM – MaaS360) in coordination with local IT
  • Ensure quality checking of all Annual Rich Media contents, provide translations and upload to the system
  • Generate and send all system related report to Supervisor
  • Reconcile number of RC and mitigate all discrepancies
  • Provide incoming gifts and correspondences to SFM and guide them for Child reply, along with CCF
  • Provide correctly translated child reply to letters for FO/NO review and processing
  • Provide contextual and accurate responses to queries on time
  • Ensure backup for smooth function in Systems
  • Ensure solution of any problems in the system
  • Tracking and monitoring standard time for services. Clear my Task meeting all requirements
  • Keep in constant contact with FO: Data Coordinator & Correspondence Coordinators

SPONSORSHIP MANAGEMENT:

  • Sponsorship KPI reporting: Facilitate Child Sponsorship reporting to ensure well-being of Registered Children and manage quality of Child Sponsorship, such as periodical CMS, SOI, Death reports are on time throughout the year with Green ratings (Horizon HOPE), if discrepancies facilitate for review, analysis, tracking and follow-up at AP level (analyse, reconcile AP Performance status, identify emerging issues and engage stakeholders).
  • RC Portfolio: Engage with Partner NGO team to manage RC Portfolio (+-2%) to ensure RC supply for Support Offices is always within standard including creating new child records and reactivation of holds.
  • RC Data Management: RC records and files are well managed, scanned and stored securely. Conduct RC Data review in Horizon Hope for accuracy to ensure all data points of new and existing RC are correctly reported in Hope. Follow the correct process for RC Data correction. Review RC ID list to ensure no Duplicates Child IDs generated in the system.
  • Child selection: The development of child selection criteria for Child Sponsorship, community participation in selection processes and mandated documentation is complete.
  • CMS: Support child monitoring processes to ensure children are monitored according to Child Monitoring Standards, under locally agreed timelines.
  • Case management and follow-up: Ensure Horizon Child-Wellbeing data (Case Management) is regularly reviewed and updated. Ensure reporting and follow-up adherence to organizational protocols. Timely and accurate reporting of Child Death and Emergency cases must be ensured.
  • Programme links: Provide analysis, interpretation of CMS, Child-Wellbeing and child participation data with AP team for appropriate case management follow-through and action.
  • Utilize the reporting and referral mechanism

SPONSOR AND CHILD ENGAGEMENT:

  • Nurture sponsor and child connections: Facilitate quality and timely communication between sponsors and Registered Children.
  • Manage Sponsorship activities to contribute to Child Participation, Life Skills and Voice: Ensure sponsor engagement feature opportunities contribute to developing life skills and promoting child participation and voice. Support implementation of sponsor communication through IPF activities as per activity designs.
  • Efficient management & delivery of sponsor engagement features: Facilitate practices and appropriate tools to ensure management of sponsor child communications (IL, SL, GN, EL, SAC, APR, CEW, SG, QMS, FL, BB and Rich Media content and sponsor queries), within Sponsorship standards.
  • Digital and Non-digital Correspondence: Ensure sponsor gifts/letters are well received from respective FO/ WV National Office (NO), verify the CCF and acknowledge the shipment.
  • Facilitate in the processing of all incoming and outgoing special mails; Sponsors Seasonal Greetings cards, Bulk cards with activity packs, Spontaneous letters, SO/Sponsor Queries Letters within standard timeline. Facilitate translation of correspondence to ensure accuracy and emotional nuance is communicated to children and to sponsors. Send all Reply letters (IL, EL, RL and GAL) to Field Office after content and translation review.
  • Rich Media Quality review: Quality review of Rich Media (CGP, CGV, CUP, CUV, YUP, YUV) products. (New SI Field Content for SFP APs)
  • CEW: Support field mobilizers to complete 100% of Child Expression Worksheets (CEW) with meaningful child engagement and quality, for timely delivery. Ensure all CEW are tagged to the correct Child ID in Horizon Hope.
  • Sponsorship 3.0: Facilitate Sponsorship 3.0 initiatives in the Area Programmes to ensure simple, modern and digital management of Sponsorship experience for all.
  • Production and delivery of materials: Support coordination of design, development of communication materials, quality assurance and administration.
  • Sponsor visits: Support DC to plan and facilitate sponsor visits, and ensure the visiting sponsors are well attended on and get wide exposure and positive impression from visit, with adherence of required protocols, such as production of the Sponsor Visit Report, Sponsor Visit CUP, Sponsor Visit-Thank you Letter.
  • Capture transformational stories: Ensure Quality Communication that captures Transformational Stories to communicate with sponsors. Support the capture, document and share lessons, good practices, innovations, impact stories, photos and videos for other stakeholders.

CAPACITY BUILDING:

  • Sponsorship capacity building: Support District Coordinator to facilitate capacity building trainings on Sponsorship standards, emerging practices in child participation, child engagement, SOI, CMS, Correspondence, Rich Media, Horizon Hope business processes, Sponsorship 3.0 initiatives, policies and Sponsorship initiatives to staff, children, community volunteers and partners – follow up its implementation and application. Enable staff of partner organization(s) to establish strong relationship with and mechanism to support children and families for active participation and engagement in integrated programmes and Sponsorship.
  • Capacity Gaps: Support District Coordinators to identify capacity gaps at Area Programme level and community and use the most appropriate training approach to address.
  • Horizon Hope Mobile App training: Ensure capacity building to frontliners on Horizon Hope Mobile App use to monitor RC. Provide orientation to new Sponsorship Field Mobilisers on relevant Horizon features and Basics of Sponsorship and its business process
  • Equip Sponsorship Field Mobilisers (SFM) and CWB monitoring committees: To inspire, equip and manage SFM and CWB monitoring committees as active agents of change for children with the required skills to meet Child Sponsorship requirements and to be emotionally available for children to contribute positively to their lives. Ensure appropriate training for emerging child and adolescent leaders.

QUALITY ASSURANCE AND RISK MANAGEMENT:

  • Child protection standards: Facilitate to ensure Sponsorship data management, quality and practices are aligned to Sponsorship and child protection policies, standards and procedures.
  • Maintain updated child information in the system and ensure data protection and Horizon Privacy Policy, as per WV Guidelines.
  • Awareness: Facilitate awareness of community, sponsors, registered children and AP staff on Child Sponsorship protocols and standards.
  • Risk management: Support in identification and mitigation of all risk exposures at AP level according to the latest Sponsorship Feasibility and Risk Assessment, and other accountability review processes to manage implementation.
  • Management of internal and external relationships required in Sponsorship operations that facilitate the satisfaction of children, families, communities, and sponsors/donors.
  • Good coordination and collaboration with FO finance, SCM, and courier for smooth and quick service.

Required Professional Experience

  • Minimum of 2-year experience in similar or equivalent positions in development sector or Sponsorship-focused organization
  • Experience in preparing and facilitating training workshops
  • Experience working in a rural or remote district of Nepal
  • In depth understanding and experience in Child Sponsorship and its contribution to child wellbeing
  • Area program work experience in Sponsorship or development projects
  • Demonstrated competencies in operational aspects of Child Sponsorship
  • Demonstrated facilitation skills, including cascading, connecting, and capacity building

Required Education, training, license, registration and certification

  • Bachelor’s degree in social science/development studies/ Sociology/ International Relation or any relevant field
  • Excellent presentation and communication skills

Preferred Knowledge and Qualifications

  • Knowledge of Sponsorship approach, systems and processes
  • Knowledge and practical skills in working with children with basic understanding of Child Protection and Advocacy
  • Knowledge of community development and engagement
  • Knowledge of local partnering
  • Effective written and oral communications skills including presentation and reporting skills
  • Good interpersonal skills and cross-cultural sensitivity. Strong collaboration and partnership skills to mobilize engagement and influence
  • Computer and Technology; Strong computer skills including Word, Excel, PowerPoint; Strong experience in using technology including information management database software and platforms
  • User experience with WV’s data management, information platforms and Sponsorship systems such as Horizon
  • Ability to work independently and collaboratively across teams.
  • High level of integrity towards resources, and identifying risks etc.
  • Strong negotiation, interpersonal skills, organizational, administrative and facilitation skills
  • Must be able to interact with diverse groups and stakeholders

Travel and/or Work Environment

  • Will be required to be based in one of WVI NEPAL’s operational district and working from partner NGO’s office
  • Will be required to spend at least 40% of work time conducting monitoring and field visits in target municipalities and wards
  • Willingness to work outside office hours especially when conducting monitoring/field visits, when hosting sponsor/donor visits and meeting Sponsorship deadline for key deliverables

Job Title: MEAL Manager

Reporting to: Sr. Education Manager

Department: MEAL

Location: Kathmandu, Nepal (with occasional travel to communities in all programme districts)

Work Hours: Full-time (40 hours per week)

ROLE SUMMARY:

The MEAL Manager is responsible for overseeing the strategic direction and operational execution of the Monitoring, Evaluation, Accountability, and Learning (MEAL) functions across UWS Nepal’s portfolio. This position will play a leadership role in the design, implementation, and refinement of MEAL frameworks, ensuring that all projects adhere to UWS standards and achieve their intended outcomes. The MEAL Manager will manage the MEAL team, guide data-driven decision-making, and foster organisational learning and programmatic improvement. As a senior leader, the MEAL Manager will liaise with internal and external stakeholders, ensuring that UWS Nepal’s impact is effectively measured, reported, and communicated.

Duties and Responsibilities

1. Strategic Leadership and Framework Development:

  •  Lead the development, implementation, and continuous refinement of MEAL frameworks, systems, and procedures across all UWS Nepal programs.
  •  Align MEAL processes with organisational strategy and ensure the integration of learning into program design and management.
  • Provide leadership in setting MEAL priorities, establishing performance indicators, and ensuring the development of robust data collection, analysis, and reporting systems.
  • Ensure the standardisation and consistency of MEAL practices across all districts
  •  Engage in strategic discussions with senior management and guide decision-making with MEAL findings.

2. Oversight of Data Collection, Monitoring, and Evaluation:

  • Lead the design of tools, methodologies, and protocols for data collection, entry, and analysis, including the use of the KOBO tool for data management.
  • Oversee the implementation of baseline studies, mid-term reviews, endline evaluations, and other critical assessments.
  • Ensure the quality, accuracy, and timeliness of data collection by MEAL Officers and MEAL Associates..
  •  Supervise the integration of Education Management Information Systems (EMIS) into monitoring processes and ensure consistent application.
  •  Identify and mitigate risks related to data integrity and propose corrective actions where necessary.

3. Accountability and Learning:

  •  Foster a culture of accountability and learning within the organisation by ensuring that MEAL findings are utilised to inform programmatic improvements.
  • Lead the analysis and synthesis of evaluation results and ensure these are shared and discussed with relevant teams.
  • Promote and document best practices and lessons learned, ensuring these inform future program designs.
  • Work with external evaluators, consultants, and research institutions to ensure independent and rigorous assessments of UWS Nepal programs.

4. Team Management and Capacity Building:

  • Supervise and mentor MEAL Officers and Associates, fostering their professional growth and performance.
  • Lead capacity-building efforts within the MEAL team, ensuring continuous skills development in data analysis, reporting, and evaluation methods.
  •  Promote teamwork, communication, and coordination among MEAL staff and across other departments.
  • Ensure proper onboarding and training for new MEAL team members.

5. Communication and Reporting:

  • Produce high-quality impact reports, visualisations, and presentations based on MEAL data for internal and external stakeholders, utilising Power BI for data visualisation.
  •  Ensure that all reporting requirements, both internal (monthly, quarterly, annual) and external (donor-specific), are met on time and with precision.
  • Present MEAL findings and progress reports to senior management, donors, government stakeholders, and community representatives.
  • Ensure that feedback from beneficiaries is collected, analysed, and integrated into programmatic decisions.

6. Stakeholder Engagement:

  • Represent UWS Nepal in external meetings, workshops, and forums related to MEAL and learning.
  •  Liaise with government agencies, donors, and partners to ensure alignment with national and international MEAL standards.
  •  Coordinate closely with the Country Director, Head of Programmes, Program Managers, and external partners for joint monitoring and evaluation efforts.
  •  Lead on evaluations commissioned by donors or partners and ensure that these align with UWS priorities.

7. Innovation and Continuous Improvement:

  • Lead the introduction of innovative tools, technologies, and methodologies for MEAL to enhance program delivery and impact measurement.
  •  Explore opportunities for integrating digital platforms, mobile data collection, and real-time monitoring tools (such as KOBO) to improve efficiency.
  • Identify and assess emerging trends in MEAL that can be applied to UWS Nepal’s context.

Person Specification

  • Master’s degree in international development, Social Sciences, or a related field (or equivalent work experience).
  • At least 5 years of experience in a senior MEAL position within an NGO, development, or humanitarian context.
  •  Proven expertise in designing MEAL frameworks, impact evaluations, and data analysis.
  •  Strong proficiency in data management and visualisation tools, including KOBO tool and Power BI.
  •  Strong proficiency in MS Office and Google Suite.
  •  Experience managing teams and building capacity.
  • Excellent written and oral communication skills in English and Nepali.
  •  Proven ability to work independently, manage multiple priorities, and meet deadlines.

Reporting and Supervision

The MEAL Manager will report directly to the Sr. Education Manager (Nepal). He/she will work collaboratively with in-country programme teams, the field team, and other colleagues at UWS Nepal.

How to apply

To apply for this position, please send a cover letter and CV of no more than two pages in English by 9th February, 2026. You should give the names, positions, organisations, and contact details of two references, one of whom should be your current (or most recent) employer.

Please also include your expected salary for this position.

Applications should be made via email to info.nepal@uwsglobal.net by 9th February 2026. UWS Nepal is an equal-opportunity employer. We are committed to our inclusive principles and do not discriminate based on gender, ethnicity, religion, disability, age, status or sexuality. We welcome applications from any candidate with relevant experience.

Position: Business Analyst
Location: Kathmandu, Nepal
Division: Software
Type: FULLTIME

Position Overview

This role is responsible for conducting detailed requirements analysis, developing Business Requirements Documents (BRDs), and translating complex business processes into structured user stories, use cases, process flows, and wireframes.

He/She will work closely with project managers, developers, architects, UX/UI designers, and QA teams, the Business Analyst will define project scope, validate requirements, identify gaps, and propose effective solutions that align with strategic objectives. Participation in Agile ceremonies such as daily stand-ups and sprint planning is essential to ensure timely delivery and alignment with stakeholder expectations.

Roles and Responsibilities

  •  Engage with stakeholders, clients, and users to understand business needs and translate them into technical requirements.
  •  Conduct detailed requirements analysis, translating business needs into structured Business Requirements Documents (BRDs) for Engineers, Architects, and Developers.
  •  Collaborate with project managers, developers, and UX/UI designers to define project scope and create detailed requirements.
  •  Develop and maintain user stories, use cases, process flows, and wireframes as part of the requirements gathering process.
  • Conduct gap analyses to identify discrepancies between business requirements and technical capabilities, proposing solutions as needed.
  •  Validate requirements by reviewing and ensuring alignment with project objectives and stakeholder expectations.
  •  Support the QA team by ensuring requirements are fully tested and meet user expectations.
  •  Participate in daily stand-ups, sprint planning, and other Agile ceremonies, contributing to the project’s overall success.
  •  Communicate effectively with both technical and non-technical team members to facilitate a clear understanding of requirements.
  •  Stay informed on industry trends, best practices, and ERP solutions to provide expert insight to stakeholders

Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree in Business Administration, Information Technology, Computer Science, or a related field.
  •  2+ years of experience as a Business Analyst, with prior experience working on ERP systems or workflow management software.
  •  Experience with Agile/Scrum methodologies.
  •  Proficiency with requirements management and project management tools (e.g., Jira, Confluence, Azure DevOps).
  •  Familiarity with SQL for data analysis and validation.
  •  Familiarity with APIs, web services, and systems integration concepts.
  •  Excellent communication skills (written and verbal) to effectively convey technical information to non-technical stakeholders and vice-versa.
  •  Strong problem-solving and critical thinking skills with proven experience in analyzing complex business problems and providing actionable solutions.
  •  A background in software development or QA is a plus, providing a better understanding of the development process.

Shanti Volunteer Association (SVA) Nepal is an international non-governmental organization committed to promoting a peaceful society where people can “Live Together and Learn Together,” while respecting human dignity and diversity. Since its establishment in Nepal, SVA has been supporting initiatives such as school construction and disaster risk reduction (DRR) education under emergency relief, community library promotion, local curriculum development, nutrition education, and the establishment of community libraries and resource centers for technical education and vocational training.

SVA will implement the project titled “Improvement of Basic Education at Community Schools through Early Childhood Education and Development (ECED)” starting from March 2026, in collaboration with local implementing partner NGOs in Lalitpur and Sankhuwasabha districts. Interested and qualified Nepalese candidates are invited to apply for the following position:

Job Title: Senior Project Officer-1

Organization/Duty Station: Shanti Volunteer Association (SVA) Nepal, Krishna Dhara Marg, Maharajgunj-3, Kathmandu
Name of Project: Improvement of Basic Education at Community Schools through Early Childhood Education and Development (ECED)
Reports to: Project Manager
Contract Type and Duration: Fixed term-initially one year (extendable upon satisfactory performance)

Position Summary

The Senior Project Officer (SPO) will serve for the implementation of the “Improvement of Basic Education at Community Schools through Early Childhood Education and Development (ECED)” project under the supervision of the Project Manager. This role ensures effective delivery of project interventions across partner NGOs, municipalities, schools, and communities by providing technical guidance, capacity building, and monitoring support. It emphasizes child-centered, inclusive, and play-based learning approaches, ensuring alignment with national ECED policies, the School Education Sector Plan (SESP), and child safeguarding standards. Responsibilities also include technically supporting classroom improvement, curriculum adaptation, learning materials development, and community engagement, and knowledge management to enhance foundational learning, school readiness, and sustainable outcomes.

Key Responsibilities

1. Technical Support for ECED and Basic Education

  • Engage in the workshop and manual content development for child-friendly teaching methodology (e.g. classroom arrangement, storytelling, learning material development, etc.) in alignment with the ECED policies and curriculum, School Education Sector Plan (SESP) and other relevant policies and guidelines as well as global ECED practices.
  • Facilitate the training, workshop and awareness raising activities for ECED facilitators, teachers, SMC and local community, in cooperation with PNGO staff.
  • Support and engage in the surveys and follow-up monitoring of the target schools and communities.
  • Support in publication of picture books, Kamishibai and other educational materials.
  • Ensure adaptation to the local context and cultural/linguistic diversity at all corners of the project.

2. Support in Project Management including Community Engagement

  • Assist Project Manager in overall project planning, implementation and reporting.
  • Coordinate with partner NGOs (PNGOs), municipalities, schools, teachers, PTAs/SMCs, and community stakeholders for effective project implementation.
  • Assist in surveys, data collection, monitoring and evaluation.
  • Other responsibilities assigned by the Project Manager.

Required Qualifications & Competencies

  • Bachelor’s or master’s degree in early childhood education, Education, Social Work, Child Development, or related fields.
  • Minimum 5 years’ experience in ECED program management, including curriculum/manual development, teacher/facilitator training, classroom support, and quality improvement of ECED centers, preferably in community school contexts.
  • Strong knowledge of Nepal’s national ECED policies, School Education Sector Plan (SESP), child protection standards, and inclusive education principles.
  • Demonstrated ability to plan, coordinate, and monitor education interventions across multiple stakeholders.
  • Good experience in documentation, reporting, knowledge management, and facilitating training using participatory/adult-learning methods.
  • Strong skills and experience in coordination, communication, and stakeholder engagement, with a commitment to child rights, safeguarding, and gender equality.
  • Fluency in Nepali and English, both written and spoken.
  • Willingness to travel extensively to remote and mountainous project locations.

Desirable Skills

  • Prior experience in multi-ethnic and multi-lingual classroom settings.
  • Experience in school infrastructure and WASH facility improvement projects.
  • Familiarity with classroom arrangement, learning material development, storytelling, reading promotion, and play-based learning.

Applications Procedure

Qualified and interested applicants are required to submit a cover letter stating their motivation to apply and an updated CV including at least two references, no later than 07 February 2026, at 17:00 via email svanepalmail@gmail.com

Only the short-listed candidates will be contacted. No calls shall be entertained

Position: Legal Specialist
Closure Date: 12/Feb/2026, 4:44:00 AM
Organizational Unit: FANEP – FAO Representation in Nepal
Job Type: Non-staff opportunities
Type of Requisition: PSA (Personal Services Agreement)
Grade Level: N/A
Primary Location: Nepal-Kathmandu
Duration: 60 working days (over 10 months)
Post Number: N/A
IMPORTANT NOTICE: Please note that Closure Date and Time displayed above are based on date and time settings of your personal device

FAO seeks gender, geographical and linguistic diversity in its staff and international consultants in order to best serve FAO Members in all regions.

FAO is committed to achieving workforce diversity in terms of gender, nationality, background and culture
Qualified female applicants, qualified nationals of non-and under-represented Members and person with disabilities are encouraged to apply
Everyone who works for FAO is required to adhere to the highest standards of integrity and professional conduct, and to uphold FAO’s values
FAO, as a Specialized Agency of the United Nations, has a zero-tolerance policy for conduct that is incompatible with its status, objectives and mandate, including sexual exploitation and abuse, sexual harassment, abuse of authority and discrimination
All selected candidates will undergo rigorous reference and background checks
All applications will be treated with the strictest confidentiality
FAO staff are subject to the authority of the Director-General, who may assign them to any of the activities or offices of the Organization.

Organizational Setting

The Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations (FAO) contributes to the achievement of the 2030 Agenda through the FAO Strategic Framework by supporting the transformation to MORE efficient, inclusive, resilient and sustainable agrifood systems, for better production, better nutrition, a better environment and a better life, leaving no one behind.

FAO is a specialized agency of the UN that leads international efforts to defeat hunger. FAO’s goal is to achieve food security for all and ensure that people have regular access to enough high-quality food to lead active, healthy lives. With 195 Members (194 countries and the European Union), FAO works in over 130 countries worldwide.

FAO Country Offices, working under overall corporate guidance and in line with the Strategic Framework, offer policy advice and support to countries in the mandated areas of FAO, facilitated through partnerships, resources and an active country programme to provide technical assistance, develop capacities and deliver core services while fully observing international standards of accountability to establish leadership and strengthen impact at the country level.

The offices deliver on the programmatic priorities of each country as agreed in their respective Country Programming Frameworks (CPFs) aligned to the UN Sustainable Development Cooperation Framework (UNSDCF) and ensure effective and efficient operations with adequate human and financial resources.

Specifically, FAO Country Offices:

(i) advocate, inform and support policies and investments in the areas of FAO’s mandate.
(ii) provide technical assistance and deliver core services.
(iii) assist and catalyse partnership and resource mobilization activities to support agrifood systems transformation.
(iv) position FAO as the specialized/authoritative technical agency in food and agriculture and agrifood systems transformation.
(v) ensure risk-smart and accountable operations and management.

The Sustainable Wildlife Management (SWM) Programme is an ambitious and innovative multi-year initiative, which began in 2017 and is funded by the European Union with co-funding from the French Facility for Global Environment (FFEM) and the French Development Agency (AFD). The initiative is implemented through a partnership led by the Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations (FAO), with the Centre for International Forestry Research and World Agroforestry (CIFOR-ICRAF), the French Agricultural Research Centre for International Development (CIRAD), and the Wildlife Conservation Society (WCS).

In 2024, the SWM Programme expanded its presence in the Asia-Pacific (AP) region, which had previously only included Papua New Guinea. The region’s exceptional biodiversity, coupled with rising population pressure, habitat degradation, and wildlife trade, including demand for wild meat, makes it a hotspot for zoonotic disease emergence. The SWM AP Project (2024–26) addresses these challenges by launching activities in countries with a history of rural wild meat consumption and zoonotic risk. The project identifies gaps and proposes targeted interventions. In addition to Nepal, the SWM Programme has recently extended its operations to Vietnam and Lao PDR.

In Nepal, the SWM Programme aims to strengthen policy and legal frameworks for sustainable wildlife management and related agri-food systems. To achieve this, it will assess the existing legal and institutional frameworks around key sectors relevant to the sustainable management of wildlife and related agrifood systems in Nepal. To frame this analysis, the SWM Programme has developed diagnostic tools on 18 policy areas: tenure systems, wildlife management, hunting, wildlife tourism, inland fisheries, timber forest products, terrestrial animal production, aquaculture, veterinary medical products, animal feed, pesticides, management of ecosystems and habitats, non-timber forest products, waste management, animal health, animal welfare, food safety and genetic resources. This process includes conducting a legal analysis of priority sectors identified by the government and making them available to the public through the SWM Legal Hub. The analysis will highlight strengths and weaknesses of the applicable legal framework and provide actionable recommendations for legal reform and improvement.

Reporting Lines

Under the overall supervision of the FAO Representative (FAOR) in Nepal, the Legal Specialist will report to one or more professional staff and receives guidance from Legal Officer from FAO Headquarter. He/she acts independently in routine matters. Supervision received is focused on the quality of the work performed and provides for on-the-job learning.

Technical Focus

The incumbent plays a lead role in the overall provision of the implementation of SWM project. He/ she plays a lead role in the provision of technical support services for successful achievement of the organizational unit’s mandate.

Tasks and responsibilities

  •  Support the preparation of a stakeholder map to help organize and lead a multi-stakeholder meeting/workshop with key stakeholders involved in sustainable wildlife management and related agri-food systems (from wildlife management and harvesting to animal production and food safety) in Nepal.
  •  Support in shaping agenda and discussing legal components and objectives of the legal analysis and help document key discussion points in the multistakeholder meeting. Based on this, the Legal Specialist assists in drafting a roadmap to inform future participatory reform processes.
  •  Conduct a desk review of existing studies/analyses on the national legal and institutional framework carried out over the last 10 years related to the identified focus areas of the legal analysis. The Legal specialist helps identify appropriate documents and policy papers by supporting desk research.
  •  Assist in coordinating and exchanging information with key stakeholders by supporting the organization of bilateral meetings and documenting contextual details and analysis.
  •  Identify and collect existing policies and corresponding legislation/regulations across the sectors relevant to the sustainable management of wildlife and related areas.
  •  Based on the overall methodology provided by the SWM Legal team, fill up the data entry backend using the relevant Legal Diagnostic Tools consisting of a list of thematic questions, and further develop per each of them brief “summaries” on the enabling legal and regulatory framework for sustainable wildlife management.
  •  Support the organization and facilitation of a validation workshop with relevant stakeholders, notably from line ministries and key stakeholders involved under Activity 3 above, to review and validate the results of the legal analysis prior to their publication on the SWM Legal Hub.
  •  Support and facilitate discussions with government partners on the need/wish for future policy reform processes and FAO’s proposed role.
  •  Conduct a desk review of studies and analyses on customary norms and practices for sustainable wildlife management in Nepal. Support in organizing the validation workshops and validation of the findings through regular consultations and bilateral meetings.
  •  Support the documentation of evidence of the successful integration of customary practices into the legal framework and the drafting of a structured analytical note.
  •  Draft a structured analytical note (10–15 pages) summarizing findings, including: Overview of customary norms; Case studies or examples from Nepal;
  • Recommendations for strengthening these practices within the legal framework.

CANDIDATES WILL BE ASSESSED AGAINST THE FOLLOWING

Minimum Requirements

  • A Bachelor’s degree in Environmental Science, Wildlife Management, Conservation Biology, Natural Resource Management, Fisheries and Wildlife Sciences or Ecology.
  •  A minimum of 7 years of relevant experience in wildlife management, conservation biology or natural resource management. (Master’s Degree with 5 years’ experience can also be considered).
  •  Working knowledge of English and Nepali languages
  •  National of Federal Democratic Republic of Nepal or resident of the country with a valid work permit.

FAO Core Competencies

  • Results Focus
  •  Teamwork
  •  Communication
  •  Building Effective Relationships
  •  Knowledge Sharing and Continuous Improvement

Technical/Functional Skills

  • Excellent written and verbal communication, facilitation and presentation skills.
  •  Good knowledge of Microsoft Office applications including Ms word, excel, power point and outlook and can independently prepare work report and power point presentations
  •  Excellent coordination and organizational skills.
  •  Experience working with government ministry and departments (Ministry of Forest and Environment, and Department of National Parks and Wildlife Management) is highly desirable.
  • Excellent report writing and documentation skills
  • Possess excellent knowledge and understanding about the regulatory frameworks and legal policies related to wildlife management and conservation.

Selection Criteria

  •  Good knowledge of computer operation of the MS Office applications, Internet and office technology equipment
  • Extent of technical and practical experience on the day-to-day operational activities of the project or programmes.
  • Communication, writing and presentation skills in English and Nepali.
  •  Ability to work with a multidisciplinary and multicultural team.

Please note that all candidates should adhere to FAO Values of Commitment to FAO, Respect for All and Integrity and Transparency.

ADDITIONAL INFORMATION

  • FAO does not charge a fee at any stage of the recruitment process (application, interview meeting, processing)
  • Applications received after the closing date will not be accepted
  •  Please note that FAO only considers higher educational qualifications obtained from an institution accredited/recognized in the World Higher Education Database (WHED), a list updated by the International Association of Universities (IAU) / United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization (UNESCO). The list can be accessed at http://www.whed.net/
  •  For additional employment opportunities visit the FAO employment website: http://www.fao.org/employment/home/en/
  •  Appointment will be subject to certification that the candidate is medically fit for appointment, accreditation, any residency or visa requirements, and security clearances.

HOW TO APPLY

  •  To apply, visit the recruitment website at Jobs at FAO and complete your online profile. We strongly recommend that your profile is accurate, complete and includes your employment records, academic qualifications, and language skills
  •  Candidates are requested to attach a letter of motivation to the online profile
  •  Once your profile is completed, please apply, and submit your application
  •  Candidates may be requested to provide performance assessments and authorization to conduct verification checks of past and present work, character, education, military and police records to ascertain any and all information which may be pertinent to the employment qualifications
  •  Incomplete applications will not be considered
  •  Personal information provided on your application may be shared within FAO and with other companies acting on FAO’s behalf to provide employment support services such as pre-screening of applications, assessment tests, background checks and other related services. You will be asked to provide your consent before submitting your application. You may withdraw consent at any time, by withdrawing your application, in such case FAO will no longer be able to consider your application
  •  Only applications received through the FAO recruitment portal will be considered
  •  Your application will be screened based on the information provided in your online profile
  •  We encourage applicants to submit the application well before the deadline date.

If you need help or have queries, please create a one-time registration with FAO’s client support team for further assistance: https://fao.service-now.com/csp

FAO IS A NON-SMOKING ENVIRONMENT

Job Specification

  • At least University degree in engineering/ science, IT/IS, computer applications or related field.
  • At least 4-5 years of experience with at least 2 years’ experience in cyber/ information security, assessment or monitoring.
  • Should have ability to critically analyze root cause of information security lapse and system failure, proactively monitor and recommend necessary security controls and coordinate for its implementation.
  • Should have sound knowledge and understanding on the requirements of ISO 27001 and PCI DSS security standard, including its implementation and maintenance. Past experience in implementation of ISO 27001 and/or PCI DSS will be advantage.

  • Should have sound knowledge on TCP/IP network traffic and event log analysis from variety of sources including individual host logs, network traffic logs, firewall logs, web logs, or intrusion prevention logs. Experience with packet analysis (Wireshark) and Malware analysis.
  • Experience in administration of Security Information Event Management (SIEM) tools and creation of co-relation rules, incident response and malware/advanced threat protection.
  • Working knowledge of diverse operating systems, networking protocols, systems administration and security technologies
  • Ability to contribute in the development and implementation of security architecture and design of various systems, infrastructure and products.
  • Ability to learn, understand and apply new technologies with strong interpersonal and communication skills. Ability to effectively prioritize and execute tasks in high-pressure environment is crucial.

Position: Finance Manager
Job Level: Senior Level
No. of vacancy: 1

Job Description

The Finance Manager is responsible to lead, manage and oversee the organization’s financials, accounting, reporting, budgeting, compliance, treasury function, procurement, administration, regulatory and governance functions to ensure strategic alignment, operational excellence, regulatory compliance, and strong financial governance in line with NCHL’s mandate as a national payment system infrastructure.

Job Specification

  • At least Master’s degree in Finance, Accounting, Management, or related field (CA/ACCA) preferred).
  • Minimum 10 years of experience in finance, accounting, audit, and compliance roles, with at least 5 years at managerial level in a company involved in banking, fintech, payment systems, or regulated entities. .
  • Strong knowledge of Nepalese tax laws, Companies Act, and NRB directives/ regulations and Financial reporting standards, audit processes, payment systems,
    Manage overall financial and investment decisions.
  • Review annual budgets and monitor utilization with periodic reporting..
  • Review monthly, quarterly, and annual financial statements with variance analysis.
  • Lead and coordinate internal, statutory, and related audits.
  • Ensure timely and accurate statutory filings with IRD, NRB, OCR, and other regulators.
  • Develop, review, and regularize financial policies, SOPs, and reporting frameworks.
  • Oversee administrative policies, procurement processes, asset management, and office operations.
  • Proven leadership, coordination, and stakeholder management skills.
  • Ability to effectively prioritize and execute tasks in high-pressure environment is crucial.

Application Deadline: 2026/02/11

Consortium of Organizations Working for Child Participation (Consortium-Nepal) is a leading network working in the area of Child Participation for more than 2 decades adopting different strategies to meet its ultimate goal of promoting child participation in Nepal. With this backdrop, Consortium-Nepal is implementing the CSO strengthening program for child rights funded by Save the Children International Nepal. The position will be responsible for assisting in all the activities of the Program and campaigns initiated by Consortium-Nepal.

Consortium-Nepal invites applications from eligible candidates for the position below:

Position Title: Associate Program Officer

Contract duration: 15 February 2026 – 30 December 2026 with the possibility of extension. However, there will be an annual renewal position as per the performance revision. Also, note that there will be a 3-month provisional period starting from the date of the contract signing.

Position Location: Kathmandu, (With a frequent visit to different provinces)

1. Key Responsibilities

  • Collect and compile good practices and documents.
  • Support in collecting relevant documents (child rights and governance) and share with the concerned partners and project team members.
  • Document the findings/learning and challenges from the monthly review and use them for the improvement of the project results.
  • Compile and document photos and videos of different events.
  • Co-ordinate with program/ project team to ensure effective implementation of the program
  • Ensure documentation of the Consortium program activities and dissemination of reports to concerned stakeholders.
  • Disseminate publications, reports, and updates of Consortium Nepal to the member organizations, Government, and other stakeholders.
  • Study the plans and policies regarding child rights.
  • Prepare success stories, case stories
  • Frequently communicate with National Child Rights Council, members, provincial committees, and Federal Committees of Consortium-Nepal.
  • Update social media pages and groups of Consortium-Nepal.
  • Facilitate documentation and reporting of project data, lessons learned, and success stories for internal and external sharing
  • Assist in administrative activities of Consortium Nepal.

2. Benefit Provision:

The Position shall get the monthly basis salary as per the organization policy. In addition, s/he shall get an amount equal to the current pay scale of 1 month as the festival allowances once a year on a pro-rata basis.

3. Supervisor and Accountability:

Program Lead

4. Required Qualification

The Associate Program Officer will require being an exceptional, dedicated, and highly motivated professional, with a strong commitment to Consortium-Nepal’s values and beliefs with the following qualifications and competencies:

  • Bachelor’s degree in sociology, media and communication/Journalism, business administration, and/or any similar field
  • Must be a child club graduate
  • Minimum of 1 year of experience in similar sorts of positions that outline expected roles. (preferable)
  • Demonstrated experience in working with partner NGOs and government agencies.
  • Proficient in using Microsoft Office.
  • Fluency in written and spoken English and Nepali
  • Strong interpersonal communication and documentation skills and sensitivity in response to Children, gender, ethnicity, and disability.

Our values and commitment to safeguarding

Consortium-Nepal is committed to preventing any type of unwanted behavior at work including sexual harassment, exploitation and abuse, lack of integrity, and financial misconduct; and is committed to promoting the welfare of children and young people with whom Consortium-Nepal engages. Consortium-Nepal expects all staff and volunteers to share this commitment through our code of conduct. We prioritize ensuring that only those who share and demonstrate our values are recruited to work for us.

Applying Procedure:

The final deadline for the application is 5:00 PM 7 February 2026. All the applications must be submitted to the following email address: vacancy.consortium@gmail.com.

Please include a cover letter with the expected salary and updated CV in the email. We are committed to ensuring diversity and gender equality within our organization and encourage applicants from diverse backgrounds to apply. Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.

Human Practice Foundation (HPF), a Danish NGO, has been working in Nepal since 2017 and in Kenya since 2018 to improve the quality of education through school infrastructure development, Learning improvement, and Economic empowerment.

Currently, we are seeking competent, experienced, committed, and dynamic Nepalese candidates for the following position to implement our learning improvement program.

Position: Learning Officer – 2

Location: Rural Municipalities of Tapleujung District

Reporting To: Learning Manager

Duration: Initially until Dec 2026, with a high possibility of extension

Job Scope: The job holder is responsible for working in a team under the leadership of the Learning Manager, and managing the learning project focused on improving the learning, vocational skills of the students and ensuring sustainability aspects of the project. The incumbent has to work with the leader schools through which the project aims to reach out to the other schools in the surrounding areas.

Key Responsibilities:

  • Support teachers in effective lesson planning, classroom management, assessment, use of teaching and learning materials, peer observation, mentoring, and reflective teaching practices.
  • Introduce peer tutoring and mentoring within the school or across the schools to encourage collaborative learning, establishing subject departments.
  • Encourage student participation in ECAs/CCAs to promote discipline, values, creativity, critical thinking, and so on.
  • Strengthen the system of school governance, working closely with the HT, teachers, community, and other relevant stakeholders.
  • Build the leadership capacity of Head teachers, SMC/PTA for a transparent and accountable governance system, encouraging and ensuring regular academic and governance review meetings.
  • Empower SMCs/PTAs for the good governance of schools and lead by example.
  • Facilitate the preparation of school improvement plans with clear milestones and measurable outcomes.
  • Facilitate and support the schools for the proper functionality of different committees and clubs.
  • Increase parental engagement in the schools through school-based/community-based parenting education sessions.
  • Promote safe, inclusive, and child-friendly learning environments.
  • Align school initiatives with the local government education plan, promoting community ownership and sustainability.
  • Support schools to identity and conduct different vocational-skill development training for the students.
  • Document best practices, innovations, and success stories for the modal schools.
  • Facilitate exposure visits and learning exchanges for other schools.
  • Ensure model schools become learning hubs for other schools.
  • Liaise with the local government for the effective implementation of the program, sustainability, and reasonable funding for the overall development of the schools.

Knowledge and Experience:

  • Bachelor’s degree in education with major in English subject (Master’s degree preferred)
  • Minimum 5 years of experience in education/learning improvement projects/program. Experience in vocational training-skill development training will be an additional asset.
  • Strong understanding of Nepal’s education system and rural education context.
  • Experience in ICT integration in Nepal and teachers’ professional development.

Skills and Competencies:

  • Strong facilitation, coaching and mentoring skills with communication and coordination skills
  • Ability to work effectively with teachers, school leadership, communities, and government stakeholders
  • Strong planning, monitoring, and reporting skills
  • Willingness to travel and work in rural areas.

Application Process:

If the above criteria and lines are appealing to you, and you have experience and commitment to contribute to change in the remote hills, you are requested to apply within 15 days (end of 12th February 2026) of this announcement by sending a cover letter with salary expectations and an updated CV with a recent photo to: vacancy.hpfnepal@gmail.com. Those from disadvantaged communities and from the project district or adjoining districts are encouraged to apply. Only shortlisted candidates will be called for the further assessment process.

Note: Telephone inquiries or any undue pressuring, favouring the candidate, shall automatically disqualify the candidacy. HPF Nepal reserves the right to select or not to select the candidates through this vacancy.
